required_complexity_patterns.notes: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root
Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words
Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute
Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span
Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Reference DOM (Emoji unicode labels): Labels contain flag emoji and unicode; Swedish field marked required Reference DOM (Table layout form): Form laid out as HTML table with labels in td cells Reference DOM (Optgroup select): Department select uses optgroup for grouping options Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: shadow-host, glued-label-text, label-wraps-deep, aria-labelledby-hidden, details-accordion. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: two-column grid without card wrapper. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Emoji unicode labels; Table layout form; Optgroup select.
